question: Under the Operate trial license described in the Availability section, what restriction applies to its use?

options.text: The trial expires after a fixed 30-day evaluation period.
Only a reduced subset of Operate features is enabled during the trial.
Operate may be used only in non-production environments, but there is no restriction on the length of the evaluation or on the available feature set.
The trial requires an active paid Camunda support contract before Operate can be downloaded.

a.text: The docs explicitly state there is no restriction on the length of the evaluation period under the trial license.

b.text: The docs explicitly state there is no restriction on the available feature set under the trial license.

c.text: The Availability section says there are no restrictions on evaluation length or feature set under the trial license, as long as Operate is used in non-production environments only. Production use is available with support in the Camunda 8 offering.

d.text: The trial license does not require a paid support contract; it is provided for non-production evaluation.

explanation: Operate's trial license is generous on time and features but strictly limited to non-production environments. Production use is covered separately by the Camunda 8 offering, which includes support.
